output_text:
S: 19-year-old woman presenting for follow-up of iron deficiency anemia. She was recently hospitalized and underwent a bone marrow biopsy on 07/21/10. She denies fevers, chills, night sweats, lymphadenopathy, nausea, vomiting, or changes in bowel/bladder habits. She specifically denies melena or hematochezia. She had a baby 5 months ago but does not describe excessive bleeding at that time. She currently has an IUD and is not menstruating. She denies tobacco use, has rare alcohol use, and no illicit drug use. Family history is significant for maternal grandmother with stomach cancer; no history of hematologic malignancies.

O: Bone marrow biopsy on 07/21/10 showed normal cellular marrow with trilineage hematopoiesis. Hemoglobin on 07/22/10 was 6.5 g/dL. Iron levels were 5. Percent transferrin was 2. No evidence of hemolysis. Physical exam: General examination performed.

A: Iron deficiency anemia. Status post bone marrow biopsy showing normal cellular marrow with trilineage hematopoiesis. Status post delivery 5 months ago.

P: Continue iron supplements. Continue Levaquin. Follow up as needed.
label: h200-nvl/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/soap_en

output_text:
S: Neurologic consultation requested by Dr. X to evaluate seizure medication and lethargy. Patient has symptomatic partial epilepsy secondary to static encephalopathy, cerebral palsy, and shunted hydrocephalus related to prematurity, with history of factor V Leiden deficiency. Last seen in neurology clinic on 11/16/2007; instructed to maximize Trileptal dose if seizures continued. Patient had a 25-minute seizure last Friday characterized by eye deviation, unresponsiveness, and posturing; Diastat was used and perceived as effective. Trileptal dose increased to 3 mL b.i.d. yesterday. Since shunt revision on 12/18/2007, patient has been sleepier than normal. Approximately six days ago (Monday), patient became more lethargic with episodes of vomiting and low-grade fevers. Vomiting stopped after hospitalization. Patient is currently lethargic and will not wake up spontaneously, though she is appropriate and interacts when awakened. Overall oral intake has been diminished. Patient is less feisty than usual. Seizure-free since admission.

O: Pertinent labs obtained here show CRP less than 0.3, CMP normal, and CBC within normal limits. CSF cultures are negative. Dr. Limon's note references CSF results: white blood cell count of 2, 1 RBC, glucose of 55, and protein of 64. No imaging studies are available in the computer; believed to have been done at Kaweah Delta Hospital and reviewed by Dr.

A: Patient with history of symptomatic partial epilepsy, static encephalopathy, cerebral palsy, shunted hydrocephalus, and factor V Leiden deficiency presenting with increased lethargy and recent seizure activity.

P: Evaluate seizure medication and lethargy. Continue monitoring.
label: h200-nvl/qwen3.6-35b-a3b/soap_en
